description_long: This sculpture depicts a classic Egyptian sphinx, featuring the body of a reclining lion and the head of a human adorned with a traditional nemes headdress. The figure is positioned on a rectangular plinth, with its forepaws extended forward and hindquarters tucked beneath its body. The facial features are stylized, with a serene expression and detailed headdress lines visible along the sides. The surface shows a weathered, stone-like texture with subtle variations in color, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The overall form is compact and solid, with careful attention to anatomical proportions and decorative details. The sculpture evokes a sense of ancient grandeur and is likely intended as a decorative or commemorative urban feature.
description: A weathered stone sphinx sculpture with a human head and lion's body, resting on a rectangular base. The figure features a detailed headdress and stylized facial features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the human face, headdress, and extended forepaws of the sphinx.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated body, detailed headdress, and the full length of the rectangular base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the headdress, the lion's hindquarters, and the tail curled to the side.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the body, headdress, and base from the opposite angle.
attribute_subject_matter: Egyptian sphinx with human head and lion body
attribute_location_context: No specific context is visible; the sculpture could be placed in an outdoor urban or park setting.
